menu_title: The Nudge -- Chasing a Colleague Who Owes You Work
menu_desc: Chase a peer without authority: soft opener, the block, a date
title_sub: Chasing Someone Who Does Not Report to You
ch3_sub: She does not report to you. And she has your file.
ch5_sub: Polite, specific, and impossible to ignore
ch6_sub: Quick fire, three role-plays, and a peer who is always busy

plan_row.tema: The Nudge -- Chasing a Colleague Who Owes You Work
plan_row.foco: Soft openers (just a quick nudge / I am checking in on) + waiting FOR + depend ON + by + date
plan_row.atividade: Nudge message + role-play with a peer who keeps promising and never delivers
plan_row.homework: Record a 2-min nudge: the ask, the block, the date, the offer of help

heading: She Owes You, and She Does Not
Care
warmup.intro: Last class you explained a decision to the CFO, and you closed like this:

callback.text: If we do nothing, we pay the same four hours forever.
callback.speak: If we do nothing, we pay the same four hours forever.

warmup.box_title: Today the person blocking you is not your boss, and not your team.
warmup.box_text: Camila, from the integration team, has owed you the API document for eleven days. You cannot start the tests without it. She does not report to you, and she is not answering.

goal.d: Three role-plays, and she keeps escaping

heading: The Language of the
Nudge
vocab_ch.sub: 8 words to chase a peer, and keep the relationship

vocab.def: A small, polite push to make someone move
vocab.def_pc: a small, polite push to make someone move
vocab.ex: Just a quick nudge on the API document.

vocab.def: A colleague at your level, with no authority over you
vocab.def_pc: a colleague at your level, with no authority over you
vocab.ex: She is a peer, so I cannot give her orders.

vocab.def: To need someone, and trust that they will deliver
vocab.def_pc: to need someone, and trust that they will deliver
vocab.hint: To need someone and trust them
vocab.ex: The whole test phase relies on her document.

vocab.def: To forget or fail to do what you promised
vocab.def_pc: to forget or fail to do what you promised
vocab.ex: Somebody dropped the ball, and now we are late.
A gentle reminder works better than an angry email.

vocab.def: To remove what is stopping the work
vocab.def_pc: to remove what is stopping the work
vocab.hint: To remove what is stopping the work
vocab.ex: One page would unblock my whole team.

vocab.def: To have a duty to give something to someone
vocab.def_pc: to have a duty to give something to someone
vocab.hint: To have to give something to someone
vocab.ex: She owes me the document since Monday.

vocab.def: To ask, politely, how something is going
vocab.def_pc: to ask, politely, how something is going
vocab.hint: To ask politely how it is going
vocab.ex: I am checking in on the API document.

heading: Someone Who Never
Delivered
vocab_check.prompt: Think of a colleague at Oracle who owed you something, and never delivered.
vocab_check.sub: Talk for 90 seconds: what did you need, how many times did you ask, and what did you do in the end?

heading: Do Not Escalate
Yet
listenings.text: Diogo, I saw your message about Camila. Before you come to me, answer one question: have you actually asked her, clearly, with a date? A message that says "any news?" is not a request. It is noise, and people ignore noise. Ask her for one specific thing, tell her what it blocks, and give her a date. Offer to help -- sometimes people go silent because the task is bigger than they said. And do not be passive-aggressive. Do not copy me on the email to make a point. If you nudge her twice, clearly, and she still does not move, then come to me and we escalate together. But not before.
comp: What does Sophia ask Diogo before he escalates?
Whether he has actually asked Camila clearly, with a date.
Why do people ignore a message like "any news?"
Because it is not a request -- it is noise.
When can he come back to Sophia?
After he has nudged Camila clearly twice, and she still has not moved.

heading: The Nudge,
Done Well
listenings.lead: This is the target. Listen, then do it your way.
listenings.text: Hi Camila, just a quick nudge on the API document. I am checking in because the test phase relies on it: without that one page, my team cannot start, and we lose a day for every day we wait. I am not asking for the full document today. I need only the endpoint list, and that would unblock us immediately. Could you get it to me by Thursday? If it is bigger than we thought, tell me and I will send someone from my team to write it with you. I would rather help you than wait for you. And if Thursday does not work, give me a date that does -- I just need to know what to tell the client on Friday.
comp: What exactly does he ask for?
Only the endpoint list -- not the full document.
To send someone from his team to write it with her.
What does he ask for if Thursday does not work?
A date that does work, because he has to tell the client on Friday.

heading: The Nudge
Message
artefact.title: One Ask. One Date. One Offer.
artefact.reason: Ask: only the endpoint list -- not the full API document
rows: The block: my team cannot start the tests without it
The date: could you get it to me by Thursday?
The offer: if it is bigger than we thought, I will send someone to help
The reason for the date: I have to tell the client on Friday
artefact.footer: Four lines. No blame, no sarcasm, no boss in copy. Impossible to ignore, impossible to be offended by.

grammar.prompt: Which one is soft? Which one shows the block? Which one is impossible to ignore?

discovery.html: "Just a quick nudge on the API document."
discovery.speak: Just a quick nudge on the API document.

discovery.html: "I am waiting for the endpoint list, and my team relies on it."
discovery.speak: I am waiting for the endpoint list, and my team relies on it.

discovery.html: "Could you get it to me by Thursday?"
discovery.speak: Could you get it to me by Thursday?

discovery.html: "If it is bigger than we thought, I will send someone to help."
discovery.speak: If it is bigger than we thought, I will send someone to help.

rows: Just a quick nudge on / I am checking in on + noun
Just a quick nudge on the API document.
My team relies on that page. We are waiting for it.
Give a date, and an exit
Could you + verb + by + day / If it is bigger, I will help
Could you get it to me by Thursday?
grammar.note: A nudge is not a complaint. It is one specific ask, one clear block, one date -- and an offer to help. It gives the other person an easy way to say yes, and no reason to be offended. Never copy the boss to make a point: that is not a nudge, that is a threat.
mistakes: I am waiting your answer since Monday.
I have been waiting for your answer since Monday.
The tests depend of your file.
The tests depend on your file.
grammar.mistake_note: Em inglês se espera FOR alguma coisa (wait for) e se depende ON alguma coisa (depend on, rely on). As preposições não são as do português.

heading: How to Chase Without
Authority
bank.sub: The soft opener → the block → the smallest possible ask → the date → the offer of help
bank.label: The five lines of a nudge
items: Just a quick nudge on the API document.
My team cannot start the tests, and we lose a day for every day we wait.
I do not need the full document -- only the endpoint list.
Could you get it to me by Thursday?
If it is bigger than we thought, I will send someone to write it with you.
If Thursday does not work, give me a date that does.

scenarios: A vendor promised the test environment last Friday. It is Wednesday, and nothing. They do not work for you.
A director from another team has to approve your budget. He has not opened your email in a week.
A peer promised you three names for the workshop, twice, in two meetings. You still have zero.
scenarios_sub: Sixty seconds each: soft opener, the block, the smallest ask, a date, an offer of help.

heading: I Will Get to It This
Week

lines.html: Camila, just a quick nudge on the API document. I am checking in because we cannot start the tests.
lines.speak: Camila, just a quick nudge on the API document. I am checking in because we cannot start the tests.

lines.html: I know, I know. It is on my list. This week, I promise.
lines.speak: I know, I know. It is on my list. This week, I promise.

lines.html: You said that last week too. I am not angry -- but my team relies on that page, and we lose a day for every day we wait.
lines.speak: You said that last week too. I am not angry, but my team relies on that page, and we lose a day for every day we wait.

lines.html: Everyone wants something from me. I have my own deadlines.
lines.speak: Everyone wants something from me. I have my own deadlines.

lines.html: Then let me make it small. I do not need the full document. Only the endpoint list -- one page. That would unblock us.
lines.speak: Then let me make it small. I do not need the full document. Only the endpoint list, one page. That would unblock us.

lines.html: One page I can do. Probably.
lines.speak: One page I can do. Probably.

lines.html: Could you get it to me by Thursday? And if it is bigger than we thought, I will send someone from my team to write it with you.
lines.speak: Could you get it to me by Thursday? And if it is bigger than we thought, I will send someone from my team to write it with you.

lines.html: You would do that? Fine. Thursday.
lines.speak: You would do that? Fine. Thursday.

lines.html: Thursday. I will confirm it in writing, so nobody drops the ball -- including me.
lines.speak: Thursday. I will confirm it in writing, so nobody drops the ball, including me.

comp: What does Camila promise at the start?
That she will do it this week -- the same thing she said last week.
Because he makes the ask small (one page) and offers to help her.
What does Diogo do at the end?
He confirms the date in writing, so nobody forgets.

quickfire.situation: Open the nudge. Soft, and specific.
tips: Name the thing. Never say 'any news?'

quickfire.situation: Say what it blocks -- in one sentence, with a cost.
tips: A day lost for every day of waiting.

quickfire.situation: She says: 'I have my own deadlines.' Answer.
tips: Do not argue about who is busier.
One page, not the whole document.
Something she can do in twenty minutes.

quickfire.situation: Give a date -- and an exit if the date is impossible.
tips: 'Could you get it to me by Thursday?'
'If not, give me a date that works.'
'I will send someone to write it with you.'
Say it warmly. It has to be a real offer.

items: Why is your deadline more important than mine?
Are you going to tell my manager about this?
If I say Friday, will you leave me alone?
pressure.followup: Ask small, be warm, and always leave with a date. Never threaten, never copy the boss to make a point.

heading: Nudge
Camila
guided.prompt: Ten minutes with Camila. Get the endpoint list.
guided.sub: Use the nudge message. Five moves, in this order:
chips: could you get it to me by Thursday?
I will send someone to help

heading: She Promises,
Again
semi.quote: It is on my list. I will get to it this week, I promise.
semi.sub: She escapes twice: first with a vague promise, then by saying everyone wants something from her. Do not get angry, do not compare deadlines. Make the ask smaller, offer help, and do not leave the conversation without a date.
chips: give me a date that works

heading: Your Real
Blocker
free.headline: Two minutes. No notes. One date.
free.sub: Take a real person at Oracle who owes you something today. Nudge them out loud: the soft opener, the block, the smallest possible ask, a date -- and a real offer of help. No sarcasm.

survival.en: Just a quick nudge on the API document.
survival.pt: Só um lembrete rápido sobre o documento da API.

survival.en: My team relies on that page to start the tests.
survival.pt: Meu time depende dessa página para começar os testes.

survival.en: I do not need the full document, only the endpoint list.
survival.pt: Não preciso do documento inteiro, só da lista de endpoints.

survival.en: Could you get it to me by Thursday?
survival.pt: Você consegue me mandar até quinta?

survival.en: If it is bigger than we thought, I will send someone to help.
survival.pt: Se for maior do que a gente pensou, mando alguém ajudar.

checks: I can chase someone without giving orders.
I say what it blocks, with a cost.
I make the ask as small as possible.
I always leave the conversation with a date.
I offer help instead of blame.

closing.line: She promised twice and delivered nothing -- and you still walked away with a date, and a friend.
closing.next_title: Day 16 -- The Line in the Sand
closing.next_desc: Setting expectations at the start, so nobody is surprised at the end

order_audio.text: Diogo sends a soft nudge about the API document. Camila promises to do it this week, like last week. Diogo says what it blocks: his team cannot start the tests. He makes the ask smaller: only the endpoint list, one page. Finally, he gives a date and offers to send someone to help, and Camila accepts Thursday.
items: He says what it blocks: the team cannot start the tests.
Diogo sends a soft nudge about the API document.
He gives a date and offers help, and Camila accepts Thursday.
Camila promises to do it this week, like last week.
He makes the ask smaller: only the endpoint list.

preclass.desc: Chase a colleague who does not report to you: soft opener, the block, the smallest ask, a date, an offer of help.
preclass.context: Camila is a peer: she does not report to Diogo, and she owes him the API document since Monday. He does not send an angry email. He sends a gentle message: "Just a quick nudge on the API document. I am checking in because my team relies on it -- we cannot start the tests, and we lose a day for every day we wait." Then he makes the ask as small as possible: not the whole document, only the endpoint list. One page would unblock the whole test phase. He asks for a date -- "Could you get it to me by Thursday?" -- and he offers something almost nobody offers: help. "If it is bigger than we thought, I will send someone to write it with you." And he confirms it in writing, so that nobody drops the ball.
quiz_ctx: Why does Diogo ask only for the endpoint list?
Because a small ask is easy to say yes to, and it unblocks his team.
Because he does not need the rest of the document, ever.
Because he wants to punish her.
I am waiting for your answer.
I am waiting by your answer.
To send someone from his team to help her write it.
To do her whole job for her.
preclass.tip_sub: Cobrar quem não é seu subordinado: leve, específico e com data. / Chasing a peer without authority.
tip_rows: Just a quick nudge on / I am checking in on + substantivo
Just a quick nudge on the API document.
Mostrar o bloqueio / Show the block
Could you get it to me by Thursday?
preclass.tip_key: Preposição não se traduz: wait FOR (esperar por), depend ON / rely ON (depender de). E o prazo é BY Thursday (até quinta, no mais tardar).

blanks.hint: Hint: a small, polite push / lembrete leve
blanks.phrase: Just a quick nudge on the API document.

blanks.hint: Hint: needs it and trusts it will come / depende de
blanks.phrase: My team relies on that page to start the tests.
blanks.post: that page to start the tests."

blanks.phrase: I am waiting for the endpoint list.

blanks.hint: Hint: deadline: ___ Thursday = até quinta, no mais tardar
blanks.phrase: Could you get it to me by Thursday?

blanks.hint: Hint: remove what is stopping the work / desbloquear
blanks.phrase: One page would unblock my whole team.

blanks.pre: "I will confirm it in writing, so nobody
blanks.hint: Hint: forgets what they promised / deixa a bola cair
blanks.phrase: I will confirm it in writing, so nobody drops the ball.

preclass.speech_intro: The five lines of a nudge. Record each one. Warm and light -- if you sound annoyed, she will avoid you.
quiz_sit: A peer has owed you a document for eleven days. You write:
"Just a quick nudge on the API document -- we cannot start the tests without it."
"I am copying your manager so this moves."
She says: "Everyone wants something from me." You:
Make the ask smaller and offer help.
Explain that your deadline is more important.
The nudge must always end with:
A date -- or a request for a date that works for her.
think: Take a real person at Oracle who owes you something today. Speak for two minutes: (1) the soft opener, (2) what it blocks, with a cost, (3) the smallest possible ask, (4) a date, (5) a real offer of help. Record it.

Now she answers: "It is on my list. I will get to it this week, I promise" -- for the second time. Answer out loud. Do not get angry, do not compare deadlines. Make it smaller, and get a date. Record it.

complementary.title: Silicon Valley (2014-2019)
complementary.desc: Richard has to push teammates who do not report to him. Watch when he asks well, and when he only gets angry.
complementary.tip: Find one scene where a request works, and copy the sentence he uses to ask.

complementary.title: BEP 418 -- Managing Conflict with Contractors
complementary.desc: A project manager talks to a supplier about late and weak delivery. She is polite, but she holds him accountable.
complementary.tip: Note the phrases she uses to refer to the agreement, and write your own version for a peer.

complementary.title: How to Lead When You Are Not the Boss: Influence Without Authority
complementary.desc: How to get people to do work for you when you cannot give them orders.
complementary.tip: Pick one technique from the video and use it this week with a colleague who owes you work.

teacher.title: Abertura (2 min): Compartilhe a tela. Diga: 'Last class you convinced the CFO. Today the person blocking you is nobody important -- and you have zero power over her.' Avise: hoje ele fala quase o tempo todo.
teacher.warmup: Warm-up falado (4 min): Toque a frase da Aula 14. Depois a virada: 'No authority, no orders. Only language.' Faca a pergunta do box e cale a boca 90 segundos.
teacher.goal: Objetivo (1 min): 'Words, one move, and then you chase me three times -- and I keep escaping.' Rapido.
teacher.vocab_transition: Transicao vocab (1 min): 'Eight words. Click each card.' Ferramenta, nao aula de vocabulario.
teacher.vocab1: Vocab reveal 1-4 (4 min): Pista, tentativa, reveal, audio, uma frase real. CCQ 'nudge': 'Is a nudge aggressive? (No -- small and polite.)' CCQ 'peer': 'Can you give orders to a peer? (No.)' CCQ 'rely on': 'If I rely on you and you fail, do I fail too? (Yes.)'
teacher.vocab2: Vocab reveal 5-8 (4 min): Mesma dinamica. CCQ 'drop the ball': 'Did the person do it on purpose? (Usually not -- they forgot.)' CCQ 'unblock': 'Does unblocking mean doing the whole job? (No -- removing what stops the work.)'
teacher.vocab_check: Vocab check falado (4 min): 90 segundos sobre um colega que devia algo e nunca entregou. Minimo cinco palavras novas. Voce so escuta e conta.
teacher.ch3: Transicao (1 min): 'Eleven days. No answer. And she is not your subordinate.' Crie a frustracao -- e dela que sai a fala.
teacher.listening1: Listening 1 (4 min): 'Sophia will not escalate for you -- yet.' Toque uma vez, sem texto. Perguntas no proximo slide.
teacher.listening1_comp: Comprehension falada (3 min): Respostas em voz alta antes do clique. Depois: 'Why is "any news?" noise and not a request?' Deixe ele deduzir falando.
teacher.artefact: Artefato (3 min): Nao leia por ele. 'Read the nudge message, then say it out loud to me, in five moves.' Ele fala; voce marca o que faltou.
teacher.grammar: Grammar discovery (4 min): Toque as 4 frases. Pergunta unica: 'Which one is soft? Which one shows the block? Which one is impossible to ignore?' Guie: just a quick nudge = leve; rely on / wait for = bloqueio; by Thursday = data.
teacher.rule: Reveal the Rule (3 min): Clique. Leve, bloqueio, data. Exija 2 frases dele com 'relies on' + 'by + dia' sobre um caso real. CCQ: 'Wait FOR or wait? (Wait FOR.)'
teacher.mistake: Common Mistake (2 min): 'waiting your answer' e 'depend of'. Preposicao nao se traduz. Peca as versoes certas em voz alta, duas vezes.
teacher.bank: Useful language falado (4 min): Cada frase lida em voz alta e imediatamente reescrita para o bloqueio REAL dele. Seis frases da tela, seis frases dele.
teacher.ch5: Transicao (1 min): 'Polite, specific, and impossible to ignore.' Suba a tensao.
teacher.dialogue: Dialogo (6 min): Voce e a Camila e voce ESCAPA: 'it is on my list', 'everyone wants something from me'. Antes de cada fala do Diogo, ELE fala primeiro; so entao clique e toque o audio. Repare: ele nunca compara prazos.
teacher.dialogue_comp: Dialogue comprehension (2 min): Perguntas sobre a CAMILA. Depois: 'What made her say yes -- the pressure, or the offer of help?' Deixe ele defender a resposta.
teacher.listening2: Listening 2 (4 min): 'This is the nudge, done well -- 60 seconds.' Toque uma vez. Depois: 'What is the smallest ask? What is the offer?' -- ele fala.
teacher.scenarios: Scenarios falados (5 min): Tres situacoes, 60 segundos cada, sem preparo: leve, bloqueio, pedido menor, data, ajuda. Nao corrija no meio.
teacher.ch6: Transicao (1 min): 'From here you chase, and I escape. Three times.'
teacher.quickfire: Quick Fire (5 min): Uma situacao por vez, resposta imediata. Sem correcao entre elas -- fluencia primeiro.
teacher.rp_guided: Role-play GUIADO (4 min): Voce e a Camila. Ele cobra usando os cinco movimentos da nudge message. Se ele sair sem DATA, mande refazer.
teacher.rp_semi: Role-play SEMI-LIVRE (5 min): Voce promete de novo, vagamente, e depois diz que todo mundo quer algo de voce. Ele nao pode se irritar nem comparar prazos: tem de diminuir o pedido, oferecer ajuda e sair com data.
teacher.rp_free: Role-play LIVRE (5 min): Sem apoio. 'A real person who owes you something today. Two minutes. Leave with a date. Go.' NAO interrompa, cronometre e diga o tempo no fim.
teacher.pressure: Under pressure (4 min): Tres perguntas duras, uma a uma, sem aviso. Se ele ameacar escalar, pare: o objetivo e conseguir a data SEM ameaca.
teacher.survival: Survival Card (3 min): 'Five phrases. With these you can chase anyone, at any level, and stay friends.' Toque cada uma; tom leve e caloroso.
teacher.learned: Wrap-up (3 min): Ele clica cada check e diz a frase que prova. So depois devolva as 2 correcoes. Homework ORAL: gravar 2 minutos cobrando alguem de verdade -- com data e oferta de ajuda.
teacher.closing: Fechamento (1 min): Homework ORAL: gravar 2 minutos de cobranca real e enviar. Anuncie a Aula 16: 'Next time you stop the problem before it starts -- you set the expectations, on day one.'
